reasoning:
The prompt states that the baseline value of (1 + the 30-day average) is 2.833. This means the 30-day average is approximately 1.83 events. For the question to resolve positively, the 30-day period preceding each resolution date must see more than 10 times this baseline — which equates to > 28.33 events (i.e., 29 or more 'Explosions/Remote violence' events). 

Achieving 29 such events in a single month would require a massive and unprecedented escalation of the Western Sahara conflict into high-intensity warfare (nearly daily drone strikes or artillery exchanges), given historically it has simmered at around 1-3 events per month. 

Recent news up to the forecast date (August 3, 2025) indicates only scattered incidents, such as sporadic Polisario artillery hitting Smara and Moroccan retaliatory drone strikes. There is no open war or evidence of consecutive massive remote violence events during July/early August, so the first period (ending Aug 10) is practically guaranteed to resolve negatively. For later periods, even factoring in rising tensions, a sudden >1500% spike in violence in a single 30-day snapshot remains highly unlikely. Consequently, probabilities are anchored at the lowest allowable bounds and scale up slightly over time to account for future uncertainty.
